Gene_Accession_Number.reason: Experimental procedures explicitly state OsUPS1 coding sequence as 'Os12 g0503000'.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: Results describe OsUPS1 as rice ureide permease1, homologous to legume UPS transporters, and Figure S2 is said to show 10 transmembrane helical domains.

Source_Species.reason: The gene is repeatedly described as a rice gene.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Rice is explicitly identified as Oryza sativa in the paper.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: For cloning the coding sequence used to generate GOS2 overexpression plants, OsUPS1 was amplified from rice cDNA of Oryza sativa L. ssp. japonica cv. Nipponbare.

Applied_Species.reason: All functional analyses were performed in rice plants.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Applied plants are explicitly Oryza sativa.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: WT and transformed plants are stated to be Oryza sativa L. ssp. japonica cv. Dongjin for the transformation and phenotypic analyses.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: Figure 1a and text state GFP signal overlapped with plasma membrane marker, confirming plasma membrane localization.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: Results state OsUPS1 is highly expressed in seedlings younger than 7 days, higher in roots, stem and flowers than leaves, and in situ hybridization showed intense signal around vascular tissues.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Results show OsUPS1 is down-regulated during N starvation and induced after N resupply; overexpressed in leaves/flag leaves/panicles of activation lines; and significantly up- or down-regulated in leaf, stem and root tissues of GOS2/RNAi lines.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: The central metabolite measured and linked to OsUPS1 function throughout the Results is allantoin.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: The paper describes allantoin as a ureide and as a nitrogen-rich product of purine metabolism.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Summary and Discussion describe allantoin as an N storage/transport compound that can be catabolized to release ammonium for amino acid synthesis and support growth under limited N.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Results report increased allantoin in panicles of OsUPS1OX, in grains of OsUPS1GOS2, and in leaves, stems and roots of OsUPS1GOS2; sink tissues are emphasized.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Direct Results support altered allantoin partitioning/accumulation, higher root ammonium uptake after repletion, increased Asn/Gln in sink tissues, and improved growth traits under low N.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed approaches are explicitly used in the paper: activation tagging, GOS2 overexpression, RNAi, qRT-PCR, protoplast localization, in situ hybridization, metabolite assays, and low-N growth tests.

Applied_Promoters.reason: The paper states a tetramerized CaMV 35S enhancer in the activation-tagging T-DNA, GOS2 promoter for overexpression, and p700-GOS2-RNAi vector for silencing.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: These treatments are all explicitly described: N starvation/repletion with ammonium sulfate, low-N soil regimes of 100/50/20/0% N, and sampling at 14 DAF.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: This is an accurate synthesis of the Results: plasma membrane/vascular expression and N responsiveness; increased allantoin in sink tissues with overexpression and altered partitioning in RNAi; enhanced ammonium uptake, amino acid changes, and improved low-N performance.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Results explicitly show neighboring genes Os12g0502800 and Os12g0503300 were not activated, and the paper proposes OsUPS1 functions in allantoin transport/loading/partitioning rather than biosynthesis.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: The Discussion mentions reduced root length under 100% N in OsUPS1OX, but this specific tradeoff is not presented clearly in the Results text with dedicated quantitative evidence; 'no major yield penalty reported' is also an absence-of-evidence statement rather than a demonstrated result.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: The paper supports OsUPS1 overexpression improving performance under limited N, so application value is plausible, but the explicit engineering/breeding recommendation and wording about 'nitrogen-use efficiency' and deployment considerations are extrapolative rather than directly tested.
